name: Azarrulla, Summit of El Hombre – circular route via Ezcaray
name_original: Azarrulla, Cima de El Hombre – circular por Ezcaray
region_intro: Mountain biking around Ezcaray offers diverse landscapes within the Oja River Valley and at the foot of the Sierra de la Demanda mountains. The region features extensive forested areas, including beech forests, providing numerous trails. Riders can expect varied terrain, from singletrack paths to routes with significant elevation changes, alongside the scenic Oja River. This area is characterized by its natural beauty and a network of trails suitable for different skill levels. Best mountain bike trails around Ezcaray The most popular mountain bike trail is Ezcaray, Pazuengos, San Millan de la Cogolla – circular through Santurdejo, a difficult 27.3 miles (44.0 km) trail that takes 4 hours 12 minutes to complete, traversing parts of the Sierra de la Demanda. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop, a difficult 34.6 miles (55.8 km) path. This route features substantial elevation gain and passes through the Valdezcaray area. Local mountain bikers also love the Sierras Riojanas and Vía Verde del Oja Loop, a 37.3 miles (60.0 km) trail leading through the Sierras Riojanas and along the Oja Greenway, often completed in about 5 hours 53 minutes. Mountain biking around Ezcaray is defined by the Sierra de la Demanda, extensive beech forests, and the Oja River Greenway. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Ezcaray are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. More than 1000 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Ezcaray's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are there around Ezcaray?
A: There are over 50 mountain bike trails around Ezcaray, offering a wide variety of routes for different skill levels. The komoot community has explored these trails over 1000 times, contributing to an average rating of 4.6 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Ezcaray?
A: Ezcaray offers diverse terrain, from flowy singletrack through lush beech forests to more technical sections with significant elevation changes in the Sierra de la Demanda. You'll find routes along the scenic Oja River and through the Valdezcaray area, catering to various riding styles.

Q: Are there any easy mountain bike trails suitable for beginners in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, Ezcaray has 9 easy mountain bike routes. While many trails feature significant climbs, you can find gentler options, particularly along the Oja Greenway, which offers a charming and less demanding ride through scenic landscapes.

Q: What are some challenging mountain bike routes for experienced riders?
A: For experienced riders, Ezcaray offers 32 difficult routes. Notable challenging options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993747773/ezcaray-pazuengos-san-millan-de-la-cogolla-circular-through-santurdejo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ezcaray, Pazuengos, San Millan de la Cogolla – circular through Santurdejo&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.3-mile trail traversing parts of the Sierra de la Demanda, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748060/valdezcaray-and-san-millan-de-la-cogolla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Valdezcaray and San Millán de la Cogolla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 34.6-mile path with substantial elevation gain.

Q: Are there any circular mountain bike routes in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, many of the mountain bike routes around Ezcaray are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993748169/sierras-riojanas-and-via-verde-del-oja-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierras Riojanas and Vía Verde del Oja Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a 37.3-mile circular trail that takes you through the Sierras Riojanas and along the Oja Greenway.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while mountain biking in Ezcaray?
A: While mountain biking, you can explore the extensive beech forests, ride along the Oja River, and enjoy views of the Sierra de la Demanda. Notable peaks and passes in the area include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4214289" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán (2,131 m)&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5225078" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Hombre summit&lt;/a&gt;. You might also pass by cultural sites like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4598570" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monasterio de Valvanera&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Ezcaray?
A: The region is beautiful year-round, but autumn is particularly stunning when the beech forests burst with color. Spring and summer offer pleasant temperatures for riding. While the nearby Valdezcaray ski resort operates in winter, many trails remain accessible, though conditions can vary.

Q: Is there parking available for mountain bikers in Ezcaray?
A: Yes, the municipality of Ezcaray provides amenities for bikers, including a public bike cleaning point. You can find parking options within the village, and some routes, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38177496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Motorhome parking – Oja River Greenway loop from Zaldierna&lt;/a&gt;, even highlight specific parking areas.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Ezcaray?
A: The komoot community highly rates mountain biking in Ezcaray, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 60 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse landscapes, the extensive network of trails through lush forests, and the variety of routes catering to different skill levels, from scenic river paths to challenging mountain climbs.

Q: Are there any routes that combine mountain biking with cultural or historical sights?
A: Yes, some routes pass near significant cultural sites. For instance, trails in the area can take you close to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2918989" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán de la Cogolla Monastery&lt;/a&gt;, a UNESCO World Heritage site, offering a blend of natural beauty and historical exploration.

Q: Can I find trails that offer scenic viewpoints?
A: Absolutely. The trails in Ezcaray, particularly those ascending into the Sierra de la Demanda, offer numerous scenic viewpoints. You'll be treated to panoramic vistas of the Oja River Valley, the surrounding mountains, and the vast forested areas, especially from higher elevations like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3546522" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cruz de la Demanda Pass&lt;/a&gt;.
tips: In the Sierra de la Demanda, the summit of El Hombre (also known as Siete Mosquejos) is 1,549 m. altitude and is crowned by a fire watch tower. It is reached from the Erizola pass.
Asphalted climb of serious percentage. The hermitage of Santa Bárbara dominates the town of Ezcaray from the hill, and is a place of worship and pilgrimage for the inhabitants of the municipality.
Mountain pass, on the dividing ridge of the Usaya and Altuzarra valleys

waypoints.name: El Hombre summit (Sierra de la Demanda)
